benchmarkRoute.answers: What benchmark-backed lean-code direction can be shown without claiming current-repo savings?
benchmarkRoute.proofBoundary: Benchmark direction is separate from this shortcut-ledger evidence and still does not measure this repo without a matched baseline.

nonClaims: Do not claim current-repo line, token, cost, or time savings from shortcut marker counts.
Do not treat shortcut marker counts as benchmark savings.
currentRepoBoundary.reason: Lean Debt counts intentional shortcut markers and missing triggers; it has no untreated baseline for current-repo line, token, cost, or time savings.

expectedAssertions: report-quality preserves the actionability question in JSON and Markdown
report-quality emits a fixtureCandidates entry for the public synthetic case
the fixture keeps scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly explicit
shareable output contains no local absolute paths or private app identifiers
the fixture exposes markerVisibilityReview.summary counts for total, visible, ceiling, missing-ceiling, upgrade-trigger, missing-trigger, upgrade-status, and omitted marker rows
the fixture exposes markerVisibilityReview.visibilityRows for tracked and needs-trigger shortcuts
the fixture keeps every shortcut marker location, ceiling, and upgrade-trigger status visible without claiming benchmark savings
the Markdown exposes Marker Visibility Review with the same shortcut rows before the raw ledger table
the fixture exposes currentRepoBoundary.perRepoSavingsClaim as not-computed
the fixture states shortcut markers are shortcut-ledger evidence only and do not prove current-repo line, token, cost, or time savings
the fixture routes benchmark direction to shipguard lean gain instead of treating Lean Debt marker counts as savings
the Markdown exposes Benchmark Savings Boundary with the not-computed claim and lean gain route
fixtureCandidate.privateDataPolicy: This materialized starter is synthetic. Use source reports only to choose report shape; do not copy private app code, local paths, screenshots, app-specific identifiers, or proprietary text.

promotion.promotionPolicy: Promotion is explicit maintainer work. ShipGuard writes starter files only to the requested output directory and does not auto-copy them into the repository.
reviewChecklist: Confirm the fixture is synthetic and public-safe.
Confirm no private app code, screenshots, local paths, tokens, app identifiers, or proprietary text were copied.
Confirm scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly remain explicit.
Confirm scoring the promoted fixture does not emit recursive fixtureCandidates.
Register the fixture in validation only after the report-quality check passes.

fixtureCandidate.publicFixtureRecipe: Create a minimal synthetic ShipGuard report pair that reproduces this report-quality question without private app code, local paths, screenshots, customer data, app names, or proprietary source snippets.
fixtureCandidate.sourceQuestion: Does it avoid pretending benchmark savings are measurable in this repo?

leanDebtLedger.description: Synthetic shortcut markers for standalone Lean Debt marker-visibility coverage.

markers.summary: use the native query parser while this bridge handles one query shape. ceiling: one query shape. upgrade: replace when repeated-key support is required.
markers.upgrade: replace when repeated-key support is required

markers.summary: keep the temporary compatibility panel during migration. ceiling: one release migration window.

markerVisibilityReview.policy: Every intentional shortcut marker should be rendered as a row with location, summary, ceiling, upgrade-trigger status, and explicit missing-trigger state when the upgrade is not yet written.

visibilityRows.summary: use the native query parser while this bridge handles one query shape. ceiling: one query shape. upgrade: replace when repeated-key support is required.
visibilityRows.upgradeTrigger: replace when repeated-key support is required

visibilityRows.summary: keep the temporary compatibility panel during migration. ceiling: one release migration window.

nextActions: Add an upgrade trigger to every needs-trigger marker.
Remove markers whose ceiling no longer applies.
Use shipguard lean review on active diffs so new shortcuts are tracked before merge.
reportQualityQuestions: Does it avoid pretending benchmark savings are measurable in this repo?

rotRiskReview.coverageBoundary: Rot-risk ranking is based on visible shortcut rows. When omittedByLimit is greater than zero, omitted markers may contain higher risk and must be surfaced by rerunning with a narrower scope or extending the ledger limit.
rotRiskReview.policy: Start with the highest-risk shortcut marker before opening source again: missing ceiling first, missing upgrade trigger second, tracked trigger watch third.

prioritizedRows.nextAction: Add an upgrade trigger that tells the maintainer exactly when to replace or delete it.
prioritizedRows.proofGuidance: Name the release, dependency, migration state, or repeated call-site signal that should trigger cleanup.
prioritizedRows.rotReason: Missing upgrade trigger means this shortcut can survive beyond its intended window.

triggerWatchContract.checkRoute: Add the upgrade trigger to the marker, rerun shipguard lean debt, and confirm rowsNeedingUpgradeTrigger decreases.
triggerWatchContract.exactNextAction: Add an upgrade trigger that tells the maintainer exactly when to replace or delete it.
triggerWatchContract.proofArtifact: lean-debt.json markerVisibilityReview.visibilityRows row with hasUpgradeTrigger=true and a non-empty upgradeTrigger.
triggerWatchContract.stopCondition: Stop if the trigger cannot be checked later from a release, dependency, migration, or call-site signal.
triggerWatchContract.triggerCondition: missing upgrade trigger; define a release, dependency, migration, or repeated call-site signal

prioritizedRows.nextAction: Watch the upgrade trigger: replace when repeated-key support is required
prioritizedRows.proofGuidance: When the trigger is true, run call-site search plus the smallest focused validation before deleting or replacing it.
prioritizedRows.rotReason: Tracked shortcut should be reviewed when its upgrade trigger becomes true.

triggerWatchContract.checkRoute: Run call-site search for the shortcut location, then run the smallest focused validation covering the replacement or deletion.
triggerWatchContract.exactNextAction: Check whether this trigger is true: replace when repeated-key support is required
triggerWatchContract.proofArtifact: call-site search notes plus focused validation output attached beside lean-debt.json.
triggerWatchContract.stopCondition: Stop if search or validation shows the shortcut is still active product behavior.
triggerWatchContract.triggerCondition: replace when repeated-key support is required

prioritizedRows.upgradeTrigger: replace when repeated-key support is required

summary.topRiskReason: Missing upgrade trigger means this shortcut can survive beyond its intended window.
summary.topTriggerWatchAction: Add an upgrade trigger that tells the maintainer exactly when to replace or delete it.

scanScope.note: Synthetic fixture; no private source tree was scanned.

scopeBoundary.purpose: Synthetic public fixture for ShipGuard report-quality behavior.

sourceInfluence.boundary: ShipGuard implements a native shortcut ledger for its own lean-code markers. It does not vendor Ponytail code.
sourceInfluence.name: Ponytail
